text: Why does the coupled Burgers-swept-KdV system tend toward compound-soliton (locked u-v coherent) states from generic ICs? Is m=0 (Gardner) the attractor, or radiative cooling, or Hamiltonian ground states, or something else?
Specifically: does the Gardner manifold m=u-v^2/2=0 attract on-manifold and off-manifold ICs to the same late-time state?
Which couplings (v_xxx in v-eq vs 3 u u_x in u-eq) are individually necessary for the compound attractor?
Is the late-time state IC-independent (universal attractor) or IC-dependent (family of attractors), and is it dissipation-scale dependent?

hypothesis_tested: H_A (Gardner-manifold m=0 attraction) vs H_B (radiative cooling) vs H_C (Hamiltonian lock to v^2/2)
design: Run identical solver on IC_A (||m_0||_L2=0.06, near-Gardner) and IC_B (||m_0||_L2=0.48, mismatched) to T=20; track ||m||_L2(t), ||m||_inf(t), lock_corr(u,v^2/2), spectral energy partition E_high/E_low for u and v, and snapshots.
method: Fourier pseudospectral Nx=256, L=30, 2/3 dealias, IF-RK4 with integrating factor for v_xxx, dt=5e-4, mild hyperviscosity on u (nu=1e-22, p=8) to prevent Gibbs at Burgers fronts.

supports_hypothesis: H_A falsified (||m|| grew in both cases, NOT converging to 0). Both ICs converged to a similar late-time state at ||m||~1.2, lock~0.3 — but this state is NOT on the Gardner manifold. Weak support for H_B (E_high_v dropped to ~10% of initial). v amplitude decreased while u amplitude grew (energy transfer v -> u via v^2 forcing).
rationale: Strong falsifier of the textbook H_A hypothesis. The Gardner manifold is REPELLING on this system at this amplitude regime, not attracting. This is the central negative-knowledge finding.

next_step: E2 will ablate individual couplings (v_xxx in v-eq, 3 u u_x in u-eq) to test whether either alone is the driver, or both are needed.
rationale: F1 ruled out the Gardner-manifold story but raised the question of WHICH coupling drives the convergence. The most discriminating next test is coupling ablation.

hypothesis_tested: H_B (radiative cooling via v_xxx is THE driver) vs H_F (Burgers self-flux 3 u u_x is THE driver) vs H_BALANCE (both needed)
design: From the same IC_B as E1, run three modified RHSs: M0 baseline, M1 (drop v_xxx in v-eq), M2 (drop 3 u u_x in u-eq). Same diagnostics, same T=20, dt=5e-4.
method: Same as E1, but with three RHS variants. For M1, evolve v_hat directly without integrating factor since v-eq is no longer dispersive.

supports_hypothesis: BOTH ablations destabilize the system, supporting H_BALANCE: KdV dispersion in v is needed for well-posedness (M1 blows up via modulational instability), and Burgers self-flux in u is needed to prevent runaway pumping by v^2 (M2 L2_u grows 4.4x). Neither alone is THE driver; both contribute.
trivial_reason: M1 blow-up is partially trivial in that removing dispersion from a KdV-type equation always destabilizes; this does not isolate radiation as the mechanism. But M2 is non-trivial: removing the Burgers self-flux while keeping forcing causes uncontrolled growth, which is the right diagnostic for whether u's self-advection plays a sink role.
rationale: Together with F1, F2 supports a forced-Burgers + KdV-radiator picture: u's Burgers self-flux absorbs v^2 forcing into propagating fronts; v's dispersion radiates excess high-k content. Asymptotic state is the dynamical balance between these two channels.

next_step: E3 will test (a) ROBUSTNESS of the compound attractor across multiple distinct ICs at the same v-mass (does the late-time state depend only on v-mass, or on other features?), and (b) NUMERICAL-ARTIFACT control via a hyperviscosity sweep (100x) to falsify the possibility that the late-time state is dissipation-set.
rationale: F1+F2 give a tentative mechanism (H_FORCED-BURGERS-SINK). The high-value falsification tests are: does the attractor universalize across IC variation (basin question), and is the quantitative result a numerical artifact?

hypothesis_tested: H_FORCED-BURGERS-SINK (working hypothesis from F1, F2); falsification probes are (a) universality across ICs (sub-hyp: late-time state depends only on v-mass) and (b) numerical-artifact (sub-hyp: late-time state is hyperviscosity-set).
design: Run M0 with four ICs all having v-mass=1.6: IC_C (random noise u), IC_D (two-pulse v, u=0), IC_E (tall narrow v, u=0), IC_F (baseline IC_B with 100x hyperviscosity). Same diagnostics, T=20.
method: Same as E1, with hyperviscosity coefficient parameterized: HYPER_BASE=1e-22 for C,D,E; HYPER_HIGH=1e-20 for F.

supports_hypothesis: Mixed: H_FORCED-BURGERS-SINK in its qualitative form survives (compound coherent structures form in IC_D, IC_E, IC_F with m != 0 and partial lock). But the SIMPLE version 'asymptotic state is a function of v-mass alone' is WEAKENED: IC_D, IC_E, IC_F all have v-mass=1.6 but differ in L2_u, L2_v at t=20 by factors 1.5-2x. Also IC_C (random noise u) did NOT converge in T=20 — basin of attraction does not include arbitrary noise on this timescale. Hyperviscosity sweep shows ~17% sensitivity in ||m||_L2(T) — quantitative late-time values are partly numerical, qualitative mechanism is not.
rationale: Multi-IC sweep is the falsification attempt; it both (i) confirms qualitative compound formation across IC variation, (ii) refutes the universality-in-v-mass strong version of H_C, (iii) flags hyperviscosity sensitivity as a quantitative caveat to be reported transparently.

next_step: wrap up: write hypothesis.md with H_FORCED-BURGERS-SINK as best current hypothesis, all falsified/weakened alternatives logged, hyperviscosity-sensitivity caveat noted, and propose E4 (long-time T=80-100 multi-IC) for the open question of whether the t=20 IC-dependence is transient or a genuine attractor family.
rationale: Three rounds have produced a non-trivial mechanism picture supported by ablation and IC variation, with explicit numerical-artifact controls. Further rounds would be needed to resolve attractor-family vs. universal-attractor on longer timescales.
